body.title: Compact cadence hook: robust session name resolution
body.result: 4-level session resolution (env→file→scan→slot-find); superclaude writes cc_session.txt; 9 tests
body.intention: I intend that the compact cadence hook correctly resolves the running CC session even when CC_TMUX_SESSION is absent from the hook subprocess environment.
body.description:
Stop hook targets wrong tmux session when CC_TMUX_SESSION is absent from subprocess env (e.g. session started before hostname-naming change shipped). _detect_session_name() finds free slot (startup use), not running session — so hook sends /autocompact to a nonexistent session and silently does nothing.

Fix: (1) superclaude writes resolved session name to cc_session.txt flat file at startup; (2) constants._resolve_session_name() uses 4-level chain: env var → cc_session.txt → tmux scan for hostname.cc.N/claude-main → slot-find.

**Affected files:** devices/claude/constants.py, bin/superclaude, tests/claude/test_cc_session_resolution.py
**Design rules:** external-state principle (hook subprocess reads session name from disk, not from env it never had)
**Scope boundary:** session resolution only; does not change fire/no-fire cadence logic or baseline tracking
**Completion criteria:** new tests pass for all 4 resolution paths; manual test: stop hook injects /autocompact into claude-main when CC_TMUX_SESSION unset
